final_set.inclusion_rationale: Auto-include: Q1 on both stable value drivers (AUM $1,127B, FEAUM $831B). Largest alternative asset manager globally. Richest strategic action catalog (3 documented actions including AirTrunk acquisition, AI data center vehicle, private wealth expansion to $290B). Pioneered perpetual capital model (46% of FEAUM). Essential benchmark for scale-driven valuation.

Auto-include: Q1 on both stable value drivers (AUM $1,030B, FEAUM $539B). Second-largest alt manager globally. Infrastructure and real assets dominance directly relevant to PAX's infrastructure franchise. Strong strategic action catalog (3 actions including Global Atlantic full acquisition, Arctos Partners, Infra Fund V). Demonstrates insurance-linked permanent capital model.

Auto-include: Q1 on both stable value drivers (AUM $751B, FEAUM $569B). Credit-dominant model provides strategic contrast to equity-heavy peers. ATLAS origination platform ($220B+ origination) and Citi partnership demonstrate bank-disintermediation strategy instructive for PAX's credit expansion. Athene retirement services integration is a case study in insurance-linked capital.

Auto-include: Q1 on both stable value drivers (AUM $638B, FEAUM $512B). Also Q1 on FRE Margin (71%, highest in universe). Demonstrates scalable operating model — achieves highest FRE margin at mega-scale. GCP International acquisition ($3.7B) and BlueCove systematic credit illustrate M&A-driven platform expansion directly comparable to PAX's Solis/RBR/WP acquisition strategy. Insurance integration via Global Atlantic provides permanent capital template.

Auto-include: Q1 on both stable value drivers (AUM $484B, FEAUM $293B). Credit Group ($407B) is the dominant segment. GCP International acquisition mirrors PAX's M&A-driven geographic expansion. 29% YoY AUM growth demonstrates rapid scaling. Q4 on FRE Margin (41.5%) provides cautionary contrast — scale does not automatically translate to margin efficiency.
final_set.geographic_focus: Global (primarily North America, expanding into Asia-Pacific via GCP)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Include as scale-adjacent cautionary case: Q1 on AUM ($441B) but Q2 on FEAUM ($169B), showing that headline AUM does not always translate proportionally to fee-earning capital. Q3 on FRE Margin (46%). Despite being a top-6 alt manager by AUM, Carlyle has been losing relative market share to faster-growing peers (ARES, OWL). Well-documented strategic actions (3 actions) and strong source coverage (4 sources). Instructive for PAX as a 'what happens when scale advantage stalls' cautionary reference.
final_set.geographic_focus: Global (Washington D.C., North America, Europe, Asia)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Include for FRE Margin signal and M&A-driven growth model: Q1 on FRE Margin (59%), demonstrating efficient fee extraction from permanent capital (85% of management fees from permanent capital vehicles). Q2 on scale drivers. M&A-driven platform expansion (KAM, Atalaya, IPI acquisitions) is directly analogous to PAX's acquisition strategy. GP Strategic Capital model provides strategic diversity. Well-documented (3 actions, 4 sources).
final_set.primary_asset_class: Credit, GP Strategic Capital, Real Assets

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Include for geographic diversity (European) and strategic diversity: Largest European-listed alt manager. Q2 on scale drivers and FRE Margin. Thematic investing approach and Coller Capital acquisition demonstrate platform buildout strategy. IPO in 2019 parallels PAX's own public market journey. Cross-border expansion from Nordic base is instructive for PAX's LatAm-to-global ambitions. Well-documented (3 actions, 4 sources).

Include for strategic diversity (organic direct investing model): Q1 on FRE Margin (61.3%) at $174B AUM demonstrates that high margins are achievable at mid-scale through organic growth and direct investing. Swiss-listed since 2006 — longest-tenured publicly listed pure-play alt manager in the set. Organic growth model (no major M&A) provides strategic contrast to PAX's acquisition-heavy approach. Well-documented (2 actions, 4 sources).

Include for solutions/advisory model diversity: $145B discretionary AUM with $958B total AUM & supervision. Solutions-focused business model (advisory + discretionary) provides a distinct strategic archetype — capital-light, technology-enabled, with 30+ year track record. Q3 on scale drivers, Q3 on FRE Margin (53.8%). Well-documented (3 actions, 3 sources). Relevant to PAX as a reference for advisory-led growth paths.
final_set.primary_asset_class: PE, Private Credit, Real Assets (solutions overlay)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Include for European credit expertise and strategic action richness: $127B AUM with strongest strategic action documentation (5 actions) in the universe. FTSE 100 constituent. SDP V ($17B, Europe's largest direct lending fund) demonstrates credit franchise scaling relevant to PAX's credit strategy expansion. Amundi wealth distribution partnership is a template for PAX's own distribution channel development. Well-documented (5 actions, 3 sources).

excluded_candidates.exclusion_rationale: Narrowly excluded to maintain target set size of 11. Q2 on scale drivers, Q3 on FRE Margin (42%). Only 2 documented strategic actions (vs. 3 for most included peers). While TPG's impact investing franchise (TPG Rise) is distinctive, the set already covers multi-strategy diversification through BX, KKR, and CG. Angelo Gordon acquisition provides M&A-driven growth instructiveness already captured through OWL and KKR.

Q1 on FRE Margin (59%) and Q2 on scale, but zero source coverage in the source catalog and only 29% metric coverage. Recently listed (April 2024 IPO) with limited public disclosure history. Three strategic actions documented but insufficient source depth for deep-dive quality. Would require additional sourcing before inclusion.

Systematic/quant hedge fund model is fundamentally different from the fee-on-committed-capital alternative asset management model that drives PAX's universe. No FEAUM data available. 41.9% metric coverage. Zero sources in catalog. Not strategically instructive for PAX's governance cascade.

Solutions/advisory model overlaps with Hamilton Lane (HLNE, included). Only 22.6% metric coverage — below the 60% threshold for deep-dive quality when using strict metric count, but note that the coverage is low partly because StepStone's capital responsibility model ($709B) does not map cleanly to standard AUM metrics. HLNE provides a more complete data point for the solutions archetype.

Zero source coverage, zero strategic actions documented, no strategy profile. 19.4% metric coverage. Despite comparable scale to PAX's aspirational range, insufficient data for meaningful deep-dive analysis.

Most direct LatAm peer to PAX but critically underserved in data: zero sources in catalog, zero strategic actions, no strategy profile, only 29% metric coverage, Q4 on FRE Margin (30%). The Vinci Partners + Compass merger (2024) is too recent for adequate public disclosure. Flagged as a gap — would be valuable to include in future runs with supplemental sourcing.

Zero source coverage, zero strategic actions, no strategy profile. Q4 on scale drivers, Q4 on FRE Margin (39%). 41.9% metric coverage but insufficient qualitative depth for deep-dive. European mid-scale alt manager but no differentiated strategic instructiveness beyond what EQT and ICG already provide.
Model incompatible — principal investor/holding company, not a pure fee-earning asset manager. Zero metric coverage. Excluded from correlation analysis.

Model incompatible — principal investor with concentrated portfolio model (Action is dominant holding). Not a fee-on-committed-capital manager. Zero metric coverage. Excluded from correlation analysis.

Only 9.7% metric coverage — well below 60% threshold. Hybrid model (own balance sheet + third-party AUM) complicates fee-based valuation comparisons. Zero source coverage, zero strategic actions, no strategy profile. Q4 on scale and FRE Margin.

Zero metric coverage in coverage_summary (0 metrics with value despite appearing in driver quartiles). Zero source coverage, zero strategic actions, no strategy profile. Q4 on scale drivers. Q3 on FRE Margin (48%) but insufficient data depth for deep-dive.

Q1 on FRE Margin (63.85%) and pure-play infrastructure focus directly relevant to PAX. However, zero source coverage, zero strategic actions, no strategy profile, and only 25.8% metric coverage. Narrow single-asset-class model limits strategic instructiveness. Infrastructure lens already partially covered by BAM and EQT. Flagged as a gap for future runs.

flagged_gaps: VINP (Vinci Compass): Most direct LatAm peer excluded due to zero source/strategy coverage. Critical gap for geographic comparability. Recommend supplemental sourcing (20-F, earnings transcripts, Vinci Partners pre-merger filings) in future runs.

ANTIN (Antin Infrastructure Partners): Q1 FRE Margin pure-play infrastructure peer excluded due to zero source coverage. Would strengthen infrastructure-specific analysis. Recommend supplemental sourcing in future runs.
CVC (CVC Capital Partners): Q1 FRE Margin and Q2 scale, but zero source coverage from recent IPO. Could be included with additional sourcing.

No EM-focused peer in final set: PAX is the only EM/LatAm-focused firm. VINP's exclusion means the set lacks a direct emerging market comparator. The analysis must acknowledge this limitation and rely on PAX's own positioning relative to DM peers.

European mid-market representation thin: Bridgepoint (BPT) and Tikehau (TKO) excluded for data gaps. EQT and ICG provide European coverage but at larger scale. The set may underrepresent the mid-scale European alt manager experience.
selection_methodology_notes: Final peer set of 11 firms selected through a convergence of quantitative driver positioning, qualitative strategic instructiveness, source coverage, and diversity criteria. Auto-inclusion applied to 5 firms in Q1 on both stable value drivers (BX, BAM, APO, KKR, ARES) — all were also in the VD-B0 qualitative list, confirming no non-obvious peers emerged from statistical analysis. Carlyle (CG) included as a cautionary case despite Q1 AUM due to relative market share erosion. Blue Owl (OWL) included for FRE Margin signal (Q1) and M&A-driven growth model paralleling PAX's strategy. EQT included for European/geographic diversity and cross-border scaling instructiveness. Partners Group (PGHN) included as organic growth contrast to PAX's M&A approach, with Q1 FRE Margin. Hamilton Lane (HLNE) included for solutions/advisory model diversity. ICG included for European credit expertise and richest strategic action catalog. Exclusion of VINP (LatAm peer), ANTIN (infra peer), and CVC (recently listed) documented as flagged gaps for iterative refinement. All excluded firms failed at least one of: minimum 2 sources, minimum 60% metric coverage (relaxed to 25% given that only 14 firms exceed 60% across the full 31-metric set — the effective threshold was applied to the 6 correlation-eligible metrics), or documented strategic actions. Model-incompatible firms (Wendel, 3i) excluded per upstream classification.
